● Background command "Verify musl target can be added to nightly toolchain" failed with exit code 143

● It evaluated far enough (just hit my 200s timeout while building the toolchain derivation — that's fine, it means the targets = [...] override is valid). The musl target can be added to the nightly toolchain via rust-overlay.

  Now let me design the flake addition. The structure I need:

  1. A musl rust toolchain (rust + musl target).
  2. A musl static rustPlatform.
  3. Rebuild boringsslPrebuilt under the musl stdenv (the existing one uses the default glibc stdenv).
  4. A packages.ubuntu22 buildRustPackage targeting x86_64-unknown-linux-musl with RUSTFLAGS=-C target-feature=+crt-static.

  The boring-sys2 cmake build needs the musl C compiler. Let me refactor boringsslPrebuilt into a function parameterized by stdenv so I can instantiate it for both glibc (devshell) and musl (ubuntu22 package), avoiding duplication. Let me write it.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Now I'll refactor boringsslPrebuilt into a function of stdenv and add the musl package. Let me make the edits.

  First, parameterize the boringssl derivation:

● Update(flake.nix)
  ⎿  Added 27 lines, removed 1 line
      83              url = "https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/boring-sys2/${boringSysVersion}/download";
      83              url = "https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/boring-sys2/${boringSysVersion}/download";
      84              hash = "sha256-RV15ll9RVdzIinq84RLDWQiDiJExt5m+2hC/moE+1mk=";
      85            };
      86 -          boringsslPrebuilt = pkgs.stdenv.mkDerivation {
      86 +          # Parameterized over stdenv so we can build BoringSSL for both the
      87 +          # native glibc dev shell and the static-musl ubuntu22 package. The
      88 +          # cmake C/C++ compiler is taken from the supplied stdenv's cc, so a
      89 +          # musl stdenv yields musl-linked .a files.
      90 +          mkBoringssl = bStdenv: bStdenv.mkDerivation {
      91              pname = "boringssl-prebuilt";
      92              version = boringSysVersion;
      93              srcs = [ boringSysSrc ];
     ...
      117                runHook postInstall
      118              '';
      119            };
      120 +          boringsslPrebuilt = mkBoringssl pkgs.stdenv;
      121 +
      122 +          # ─── Static musl build for portable Linux deployment (e.g. Ubuntu 22) ───
      123 +          # The currently-deployed binary is a static-pie musl ELF with zero
      124 +          # glibc symbol versions, so it runs on any Linux regardless of the
      125 +          # host glibc. We reproduce that here as a flake output so the recipe
      126 +          # is no longer ad-hoc: `nix build .#ubuntu22`.
      127 +          muslPkgs = pkgs.pkgsCross.musl64;
      128 +          muslTarget = "x86_64-unknown-linux-musl";
      129 +          muslRust = pkgs.rust-bin.selectLatestNightlyWith (toolchain: toolchain.default.override {
      130 +            extensions = [ "rust-src" ];
      131 +            targets = [ muslTarget ];
      132 +          });
      133 +          # Static stdenv: links the C/C++ runtime statically so BoringSSL's
      134 +          # .a files (and the final binary) carry no dynamic libc dependency.
      135 +          muslStdenv = muslPkgs.stdenvAdapters.useMoldLinker muslPkgs.stdenv;
      136 +          boringsslPrebuiltMusl = mkBoringssl muslStdenv;
      137 +          muslRustPlatform = pkgs.makeRustPlatform {
      138 +            rustc = muslRust;
      139 +            cargo = muslRust;
      140 +            stdenv = muslStdenv;
      141 +          };
      142          in
      143          {
      144            _module.args.pkgs = pkgs;

● Now add the packages.ubuntu22 output. I need to set the cargo target, crt-static, the cross C compiler for any C deps, and BORING_BSSL_PATH to the musl boringssl. The repo's .cargo/config.toml only sets rustflags for the x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu target, so the musl target won't inherit the mold/tokio_unstable flags —
  but the code needs --cfg tokio_unstable. I must supply those via env for the musl target too.

● Update(flake.nix)
  ⎿  Added 30 lines
      157              src = pkgs.lib.cleanSource ./.;
      158            };
      159
      160 +          # Fully-static x86_64 musl binary for deployment on glibc hosts
      161 +          # (Ubuntu 22.04 etc.). No dynamic libc → runs anywhere.
      162 +          #   nix build .#ubuntu22 && ./result/bin/zero_fee_arb
      163 +          packages.ubuntu22 = muslRustPlatform.buildRustPackage {
      164 +            pname = "${pname}-ubuntu22";
      165 +            version = manifest.version;
      166 +
      167 +            nativeBuildInputs = with pkgs; [ pkg-config cmake perl ];
      168 +
      169 +            cargoBuildFlags = [ "-p" "zero_fee_arb" "--target" muslTarget ];
      170 +            doCheck = false; # cross target: host can't necessarily run musl test bins under the sandbox
      171 +
      172 +            cargoLock.lockFile = ./Cargo.lock;
      173 +            src = pkgs.lib.cleanSource ./.;
      174 +
      175 +            # Link the C runtime statically so the result has no libc.so dep.
      176 +            CARGO_BUILD_TARGET = muslTarget;
      177 +            CARGO_TARGET_X86_64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_MUSL_LINKER = "${muslStdenv.cc}/bin/cc";
      178 +            # The repo .cargo/config.toml only injects these cfgs for the gnu
      179 +            # target; the code relies on tokio_unstable at compile time, so we
      180 +            # must pass them (plus crt-static) for the musl target explicitly.
      181 +            CARGO_BUILD_RUSTFLAGS = "-C target-feature=+crt-static --cfg tokio_unstable --cfg web_sys_unstable_apis";
      182 +            # boring-sys2 links the prebuilt musl BoringSSL and skips its own
      183 +            # patch+cmake step (see mkBoringssl note above).
      184 +            BORING_BSSL_PATH = "${boringsslPrebuiltMusl}";
      185 +            # cc crate / cmake for any C deps must target musl.
      186 +            CC_x86_64_unknown_linux_musl = "${muslStdenv.cc}/bin/cc";
      187 +            CXX_x86_64_unknown_linux_musl = "${muslStdenv.cc}/bin/c++";
      188 +          };
      189 +
      190            devenv.shells.default = {
      191              languages.python = {
      192                enable = true;

● Now let me build it. This will be a long compile (heavy dep tree + BoringSSL). Let me run it in the background and monitor.

● Bash(cd /home/v/s/zero_fee_arb; nix build .#ubuntu22 --print-build-logs 2>&1 | tee /tmp/zfa_musl_build.log | tail -5; echo "[EXIT $?]")
  ⎿  Running in the background (↓ to manage)
